text:
One afternoon, young Sam sat sprawled on the grass in a quiet suburban park. The sun was shining, casting a warm glow over the lush green surroundings, and bees buzzed from blossom to blossom.

Sam had been reading about birds. Their songs, migrations, behaviors fascinated him. Today he was curious about the great tit. He saw two high-flying birds whizzing past; their small bodies seemed almost ethereal against the blue sky.

He stood up, stretching his arms high over his head. He hummed a tune a little like the birds he'd read about. "Did you hear that? It sounded like a triumphant song!"

He made his way through the meadow, listening closer. Birds were everywhere. He counted eight species. Each had its unique song, a melody that could recognize friends across distances.

The great tits' songs were particularly impressive—they sang from their lofty perch near the beech trees. Sam watched as one paused in its flight, plucking a seed
